description: This micrograph shows the cross-section of a large oxide nodule formed on Fe-Mn-Al alloy after 24 h isothermal oxidation at 800 °C under 200 torr pure oxygen; the caption specifies the alloy composition as Fe-7.5Mn-3Al, while associated supporting text cites this figure for oxide nodules formed on Fe-7.5Mn-5Al alloy. The bright region at the lower left is the Fe-Mn-Al alloy substrate, the dark upper region is the embedding mounting medium, and the heterogeneous, porous layered structure between them is the grown oxide nodule with visible internal voids distributed across the oxide layer. Text reports this nodule is structurally similar to nodules formed on binary Fe-Al alloys, consisting of an outer white Fe₂O₃ layer, an inner Fe₃O₄ layer, with Mn and Al enrichment detected in the inner Fe₃O₄ layer and at the gas-oxide interface, plus Mn₂O₃ particles present in the outer Fe₂O₃ layer. This figure supports that oxide nodules formed on Fe-Mn-Al alloys with insufficient Al content to form continuous protective alumina scales have phase compositions strongly dependent on Mn alloying content.
